title: Project Manager Online Resources (f/m/d)
required_skills: Bachelor's degree or equivalent; ideally with project management training
Strong project management and organisational skills
Collaborative and effective in matrix organisations without formal authority
Proficiency in at least one additional modern language (plus)
matched_skills: Bachelor's degree in Management Administration (South China University of Technology) — matches degree requirement
Project management skills: cross-functional project coordination at Hapag-Lloyd (agile sprints, deliverables), HSBC (6-workstream programme)
Organisational skills: milestone tracking, documentation, meeting facilitation across multiple roles
Communication skills (written and spoken): executive reporting, presentation preparation, internal communications
Matrix organisation effectiveness: coordinated Product/Engineering/Marketing/C-suite at HSBC without formal authority
Additional languages: German (B1), Chinese (C2), Cantonese (C2) — meets multilingual bonus requirement strongly
Budget management: USD 8M programme budget variance reporting at HSBC
Vendor/partner coordination: scheduling, logistics, supplier management across roles
missing_skills: Publishing industry experience (no background in academic publishing or editorial workflows)
Content quality assurance for publication standards
Familiarity with digital publishing production workflows
core_responsibilities: Convert publishing plans into detailed project plans including budgets, timelines, and resource planning
Coordinate editorial teams and external partners, monitor progress
Manage project budgets and flag overruns
Guide and support authors and editors
Ensure timely handover to Production/Platform Manager
recommended_emphasis: Lead with cross-functional project coordination experience (Hapag-Lloyd and HSBC) as direct evidence of matrix organisation PM capability
Highlight multilingual skills (English C1, German B1, Chinese C2) as a strong asset for an international academic publisher
Emphasise documentation, governance, and reporting experience from HSBC as evidence of project structure discipline
Note the fixed-term 2-year nature is a fit for the candidate's availability and international profile
summary: Match score 66.7% — moderate match. The candidate's cross-functional project coordination, matrix organisation experience, strong communication skills, and multilingual profile (especially Chinese C2 which is rare) map well to this project management role in academic publishing. The primary gap is the absence of publishing domain knowledge, but this role appears open to candidates without specialised publishing backgrounds. Worth applying — the multilingual profile and PM fundamentals are genuine differentiators.
